title: torilis pseudonodosa
intro: Torilis pseudonodosa, also known as Wild Chervil or Horned Parsley, is a biennial herb native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, characterized by its fern-like leaves and small white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a carminative and diuretic, helping to ease digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote urination. The health benefits of Torilis pseudonodosa include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of arthritis and gout. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
